'''Christopher Atkin''' (born 7 February 1993) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh|hk}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Swinton Lions]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Atkin also played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Championship and the [[Super League]], and for [[Salford Red Devils]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the Super League. He has represented the [[England Knights]] at international level.

==Background==
Atkin was born in [[Widnes]], Cheshire, England. He is a graduate of [[Liverpool John Moores University]]. Atkin is also a qualified [[teacher]], previously teaching [[Physical Education]] at [[Wade Deacon High School]] in [[Widnes]].

==Early career==
Atkin was in the [[Widnes Vikings]]' Academy System and he has previously played for the England Students in the [[Tertiary Student Rugby League World Cup|2013 Student Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Widnes rugby youngster Chris Atkin hoping for World Cup future">{{cite web|url=http://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/widnes-rugby-youngster-chris-atkin-4279751|title=Widnes rugby youngster Chris Atkin hoping for World Cup future|publisher=Liverpool Echo|date=8 June 2013|accessdate=14 October 2016}}</ref> Atkin was then eventually released by the Widnes Academy System due to them already having two established young halves in [[Tom Gilmore (rugby league)|Tom Gilmore]] and [[Danny Craven (rugby league)|Danny Craven]].

==Senior career==
[[File:Chris Atkin.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Atkin playing for the Swinton Lions in 2017]]

===Swinton Lions (2014–17)===
Between 2014 and 2017, he played for [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] on a [[Part-time job|part-time]] basis under head coach [[John Duffy (rugby league)|John Duffy]]. Atkin was the hero in the Swinton's 2015 play-off semi-final victory over the [[York City Knights]], slotting over the match-winning drop-goal in golden-point extra-time, to secure their spot in the 2015 play-off final.<ref name="Hero Chris Atkin puts Swinton Lions in the Grand Final with last-ditch win over York">{{cite web |date=28 September 2015 |title=Hero Chris Atkin puts Swinton Lions in Grand Final with last-ditch win over York |url=http://salfordonline.com/9476-hero-chris-atkin-puts-swinton-lions-in-grand-final-with-last-ditch-win-over-york.html |accessdate=14 October 2016 |publisher=Salford Online}}</ref> Once again, Atkin's impressive performance in the 2015 play-off final against the [[Keighley Cougars]] proved to be the difference at the final whistle. Swinton gained promotion to the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] with a thrilling 29–28 victory over Keighley,<ref name="Atkin looking to make the most of Whitehaven game">{{cite web |date=5 March 2016 |title=Atkin looking to make the most of Whitehaven game |url=http://www.totalrl.com/chris-atkin-swinton-lions-preview |accessdate=14 October 2016 |publisher=Total RL}}</ref> with Atkin scoring a try and kicking over a drop-goal, which proved to be the deciding factor come the end of the match.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2017–19)===
Atkin was originally slated to join [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the 2018 season, but due to financial difficulties at Swinton, Hull Kingston Rovers agreed to pay the Swinton club a five-figure transfer fee to secure Atkin's services with immediate effect during the dying stages of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr/hull-kr-s-financial-pledge-to-fast-track-chris-atkin-deal-1-8642120|title= Hull KR's financial pledge to fast-track Chris Atkin deal|work=The Yorkshire Post|date=10 July 2017|accessdate=26 September 2018}}</ref> On 23 July 2017, Atkin made his Hull Kingston Rovers début against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], in a 28-6 [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] league defeat.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/halifax-vs-hull-kr-live-229936|title=Halifax vs Hull KR LIVE – Score updates, match action, pictures and reaction|work=Hull Daily Mail|date=23 July 2017|accessdate=26 September 2018}}</ref> Atkin was part of the Hull Kingston Rovers side that won promotion back to the [[Super League]] at the first time of asking following relegation the season prior.

Following an impressive start to his Hull Kingston Rovers career in the latter-stages of the 2017 [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] season, Atkin was quickly handed his [[Super League]] bow in round two against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in 2018, where he won the [[Sky Sports]] 'Man-of-the-Match' Award.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-hull-k-r/48276|title=Leeds 20-11 Hull KR: Rhinos edge spirited Rovers in Super League|work=Sky Sports|date=9 February 2018|accessdate=26 September 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-hull-k-r/live/48276|title=Live Commentary|work=Sky Sports|date=8 February 2018|accessdate=26 September 2018}}</ref> Atkin scored his first try for Hull Kingston Rovers on 2 April 2018, in a 44–6 Super League defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[DW Stadium]].

===Salford Red Devils (2020–25)===
In round 10 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Atkin kicked the winning field goal for Salford in their 9–8 victory over Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57499903|title=Huddersfield Giants 8-9 Salford Red Devils|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

In the 2022 Super League season, Atkin played 20 games for Salford including their semi-final loss against St Helens.

In the 2023 Super League season, Atkin played 27 games for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers (2025–26)===
On 29 April 2025, Atkin signed for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=29 Apr 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Chris Atkin breaks silence following Salford Red Devils exit and Castleford Tigers move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/chris-atkin-breaks-silence-following-salford-red-devils-exit-and-castleford-tigers-move/}}</ref>

=== Bradford Bulls (2026–) ===
On 18 March 2026, Atkin signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] on a deal until the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Atkinson |first=Nathan |date=18 March 2026 |title=Bradford Bulls sign Chris Atkin from Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/25946737.bradford-bulls-sign-chris-atkin-castleford-tigers/ |access-date=18 March 2026 |website=The Telegraph & Argus}}</ref>

==Representative career==

===England Knights (2018)===
===2018===
It was announced by the [[Rugby Football League]] on 7 March 2018, that Atkin had been selected in the 25-man [[England Knights]]' Performance Squad, that would be touring [[Papua New Guinea]] for a two-game [[Test match (rugby league)|Test match]] series later in the year.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>

Atkin made his début for the [[England Knights]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] on 27 October 2018, the game played in [[Lae]] ended in a 12-16 victory to the [[England Knights|Knights]].<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref> He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea v England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea v England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref>
